title: amorphophallus albus
intro: Amorphophallus albus, also known as the white cobra lily or white dracena, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the arum family. Native to Sri Lanka, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Amorphophallus albus include its ability to treat respiratory issues, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its use should be strictly controlled due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
